name: Peña Chana y Laguna de las Verdes – circular desde Torre de Babia
region_intro: Hiking around Cabrillanes offers access to diverse landscapes within the Babia Biosphere Reserve, a protected area known for its ecological significance. The region features high mountain terrain, evidence of past glacial activity, and river sources. Hikers can explore valleys, pastures, and peaks, including those within the Ubiña massif. This area provides varied geological and ecological features suitable for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Cabrillanes The most popular hiking route is Peña Orniz through the Fuentes del Sil - from La Cueta through the Babia y Luna Natural Park, a 9.5 miles (15.3 km) trail that takes 6 hours 7 minutes to complete. This difficult route traces the initial section of the Sil river to its source at the base of Peña Orniz. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peña Chana y Laguna de las Verdes – circular desde Torre de Babia, a difficult 9.0 miles (14.5 km) path. This circular route leads to the Laguna de Las Verdes, offering high mountain views. Local hikers also love the Lumajo Waterfall Circular, a 4.4 miles (7.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain to a waterfall, often completed in about 2 hours 24 minutes. Hiking around Cabrillanes is defined by high mountain landscapes, glacial formations, and river valleys. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Cabrillanes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from nearly 400 reviews. More than 3,100 hikers have used komoot to explore Cabrillanes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Cabrillanes?
A: Cabrillanes offers a wide range of hiking opportunities, with over 80 trails available for exploration. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What makes hiking in Cabrillanes unique?
A: Hiking in Cabrillanes provides access to the stunning Babia Biosphere Reserve, a protected area known for its exceptional natural beauty. You'll encounter diverse landscapes, including high mountain terrain, evidence of past glacial activity, and the sources of rivers like the Sil. The region offers impressive panoramic views, especially from areas within the Ubiña massif.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Cabrillanes?
A: Yes, Cabrillanes has several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. There are 7 routes classified as easy, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty without significant challenge.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Cabrillanes?
A: Yes, Cabrillanes features several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051271198/pena-chana-y-laguna-de-las-verdes-circular-desde-torre-de-babia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Chana y Laguna de las Verdes – circular desde Torre de Babia&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 14.5 km loop offering high mountain views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051972509/lumajo-waterfall-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lumajo Waterfall Circular&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.1 km trail that takes you through varied terrain to a beautiful waterfall.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in Cabrillanes?
A: Yes, you can discover trails that lead to waterfalls in Cabrillanes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051972509/lumajo-waterfall-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lumajo Waterfall Circular&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.1 km route specifically designed to take you to a waterfall. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40014947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palace of Quiñones – San Bartolo Waterfall loop from Riolago de Babia&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 12.8 km loop.

Q: What kind of landmarks or natural features can I see along the trails?
A: Along the trails in Cabrillanes, you can encounter various natural and historical landmarks. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6332683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Braña de Mumián&lt;/a&gt;, a historical site, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/176266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventana Pass&lt;/a&gt;. You might also come across the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3387526" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of the Valley&lt;/a&gt;. The region itself is characterized by glacial formations and high mountain landscapes within the Babia Biosphere Reserve.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Cabrillanes?
A: The trails in Cabrillanes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from nearly 400 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning high mountain landscapes, the evidence of past glacial activity, and the diverse river valleys. Over 3,100 hikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, indicating a strong appreciation for the region's natural beauty.

Q: Are there any routes that follow river sources?
A: Yes, one notable route traces the initial section of a significant river. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041200568/pena-orniz-through-the-fuentes-del-sil-from-la-cueta-through-the-babia-y-luna-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Orniz through the Fuentes del Sil&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 15.3 km trail that follows the Sil river to its source at the base of Peña Orniz (2,191m).

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty hikes in Cabrillanes?
A: Yes, a significant number of trails in Cabrillanes are of moderate difficulty, offering a good balance of challenge and accessibility. There are 47 moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14987115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Verdes Mountain Pasture – Lagoon of Las Verdes loop from Torre de Babia&lt;/a&gt;, an 11 km loop that takes you through beautiful landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Cabrillanes?
A: Given its high mountain landscapes and potential for glacial formations, the best time to hike in Cabrillanes is typically during the warmer months, from late spring to early autumn. This period offers more favorable weather conditions and ensures trails are clear of snow, allowing full access to the diverse terrain and stunning views of the Babia Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Are there any historical sites or traditional villages to explore near the trails?
A: Yes, the region combines nature with culture and tradition. The village of Riolago de Babia, for instance, is known for its palaces and coat-of-arms adorned houses, reflecting a rich heritage of transhumance traditions and local legends. You can explore these historical sites as part of your hiking experience.

Spectacular picture that we find in the Laguna de las Verdes. The walls of Pico Montihuero next to the lagoon create a dream place. Without a doubt, we will take out our camera to photograph.
With views of Peña Chana, the Majada de las Verdes gives us a picturesque picture. A real pleasure for lovers of the mountains and hiking.
